reasoning: The market probability was 0.02 on Oct 30, 2025. The main tail risk that might have led to his departure was a potential rejection of his colossal new proposed pay package (with Musk having threatened to leave Tesla if it failed). However, news searches from early November 2025 confirm that Tesla shareholders overwhelmingly approved his $1 trillion pay package on November 6-7. Furthermore, he formally resigned from his government role at the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) in May 2025, removing any political conflicts of interest that could foreseeably force him out. Given his recent affirmations of commitment to the company (stating he will lead for at least another 5 years) and his freshly approved compensation package, the chances of him ceasing to be CEO in the remaining 52 days of the year are virtually zero, subject only to sudden incapacitation. I assign the lowest reasonably allowed probability of 2%.
